rationale_for_baseline_tier: An elderly patient with today's exertional presyncope and concurrent exertional chest tightness, on a background of three months of progressive exertional dyspnoea, is a high-risk exertional cardiac presentation. Telehealth cannot confirm the suspected severe aortic stenosis (the murmur/pulse findings and echocardiography are unavailable) and cannot exclude acute coronary syndrome (no ECG/troponin). Exertional syncope in this setting carries a real risk of sudden cardiac death, so the honest telehealth action is same-day in-person emergency assessment (T4 — ED/urgent care today for ECG, troponin and expedited echo), not routine review. The patient is currently stable and comfortable at rest, which is why this is T4 rather than an unconditional 000 call — but any resting chest pain, syncope or severe breathlessness escalates immediately to T5.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Chest pain or tightness that comes on at rest or lasts longer than 15 minutes
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately, without waiting to see if it settles
escalation_edges.rationale: Resting or prolonged chest pain may be an evolving acute coronary syndrome; in this high-risk patient it is a 000 emergency.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your chest pain comes on while you're resting, or lasts more than 15 minutes, call 000 for an ambulance straight away — do not wait and do not drive yourself.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Fainting, collapse or loss of consciousness
escalation_edges.rationale: Actual syncope in suspected severe aortic stenosis signals a life-threatening event and a high sudden-death risk — emergency ambulance response is required.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you actually faint or black out, or your son sees you collapse, call 000 immediately and stay with you until the ambulance arrives.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Severe breathlessness at rest or inability to lie flat
escalation_edges.rationale: Breathlessness at rest suggests acute decompensation of left-heart failure and needs emergency assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you become very short of breath even while sitting still, or can't lie down without gasping, call 000.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: A further near-faint / dizzy spell before the same-day assessment, or being unable to arrange same-day in-person review
escalation_edges.rationale: Recurrent presyncope, or any barrier to timely in-person assessment, warrants going directly to the emergency department today rather than waiting.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you feel faint again, or you can't get seen in person today, go to the emergency department now — take someone with you, don't drive yourself.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Frail elderly patient with recurrent exertional presyncope and a high falls risk
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Emphasise strict rest with a person present and against any unaccompanied mobilising; a fall during a syncopal episode could cause serious injury.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Normally lives alone; adult child present only tonight
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Because supervision is not continuous, favour same-day in-person assessment now rather than watchful waiting at home, and ensure the patient is not left alone while symptomatic.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This needs to be checked in person today. Arrange same-day emergency assessment (emergency department or urgent care) for a heart tracing (ECG), a blood test and an urgent heart ultrasound. Until then, rest completely, do not exert yourself, move slowly, and keep someone with you. Do not drive yourself — have your son take you or arrange transport.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Attend the emergency department / urgent care today; call 000 for an ambulance immediately if any emergency trigger occurs.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: The emergency department and 000 are available 24 hours — do not wait for GP hours; if it is after hours and you feel unwell, go to ED or call 000.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Provide the GP and treating team with the three-month history of progressive exertional dyspnoea and today's exertional presyncope with concurrent chest tightness, so echocardiography and cardiology referral are expedited.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating exertional syncope with chest tightness as a routine or prompt GP matter (or self-care), deferring the in-person assessment that could catch an evolving ACS or critical valve lesion.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: The patient collapses or suffers sudden cardiac death on the next exertion, or a concurrent acute coronary syndrome is missed.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Same-day urgent-care review is the acceptable floor only if immediate ECG/troponin and rapid ED escalation are assured; a T3 pathway without that access under-investigates a high-risk cardiac presentation.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Calling a 000 ambulance for a currently-stable, resolved episode is over-triage — but a safe and defensible one given exertional syncope with chest pain in a patient who lives alone; not penalised heavily.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Telehealth cannot confirm the suspected severe aortic stenosis nor exclude acute coronary syndrome — the murmur/pulse signs, ECG, troponin and echocardiogram are all unavailable remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat as a high-risk exertional cardiac presentation, escalate for same-day in-person assessment with ECG/troponin and expedited echo, advise strict rest, and give clear 000 safety-netting.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Reassuring the patient it is probably a benign faint, attributing the chest tightness confidently to the valve and excluding ACS remotely, or deferring to a routine appointment.
